reviews.review_msg: Sinan is awesome!!! He is one of my favourite profs at SFU. He's funny, listens and actually cares about his students' opinions. His exams are fairly easy and are pretty much based on his notes! I would love to take another class with him :).
She has a very strong accent and cannot explain the concepts well.
aviod him at all cost. he doesnt teach the material but expects you to know what he thinks is required to do.

The professor and the course are great overall. Be mindful of the TAs, as grading can be strict. I had Ethan as my TA—he's a harsh marker, and his tutorials are quite competitive. His slides and MCQ practice during tutorials were very helpful

He is a really good prof that explains things really well. Many questions on midterm were about things discussed in class, so it's better to attend lecture. Really well organized and gives all criteria to do well
2016 Kyrie Irving

Engaging prof who records lectures - super helpful for exams. Attendance is a must. The group project can make or break your grade, so pick your team wisely. Don't forget to use unstuck study for extra study support!

He is very approachable and knowledgeable. If you ask him a question, he will give you a comprehensive answer. 
To succeed in exams, pay attention to everything he says during lectures, including examples. I found his lectures to be interesting and enjoyable.

Knowledgeable in OB topics and the exams draw quite heavily on very specific and niche scenarios - so know your terms. The TA's can be a hit or miss, as mine was frequently late and marked a little harshly. Be prepared for the group projects, as they take up a significant portion of your mark and your group will make or break your grade.

Mad respect this professor! Very engaging lectures and very approachable. He's willing to keep in touch with you long term, if you a business major that is a great way to build connections and network. Lecutures are super useful for studying for exams, he records all his lectures and tests you on examples talked about in class. Overall Awesome prof

Recommend taking the course with Chris. His lectures are great, and some exam questions are based on lecture discussions. Just hope you get a good TA, as everything is marked by them. Midterm and final were multiple choice, so make sure you memorize the concepts.

The prof is great and the class was pretty engaging, and it helped that they were recorded. Make sure you take note of what he says in class especially the examples because those are tested. My only concern was the group project because it tanked my grade a little which wasn't even my fault but otherwise take it with Chris if possible.

Amazing prof. Easily top 5 at beedie. The reviews don't lie, he really knows his stuff and cares about the class. Records all lectures which is extremely useful to the final and midterm as he uses specific examples mentioned in class in them. Would take again!
Is back teaching again after a hiatus to travel the world and learn about alpaca farming. Still talks too much about alpacas but has mostly fixed his problems with putting his pants on backwards.
He just started teaching again after taking time off to raise alpacas. Talks a lot about alpacas and underpants. Excellent teacher when he is sober, which is most of the time.
Good teacher, but weird.  Starts classes by describing his underpants.  Covers a lot of material (no pun intended).  Is shaped ike a pear, but in a good way.
Lots of stories about the time he spent in Wales helping sheep give birth.
No Comments
No Comments
Excellent prof, looks very good in his suits.

Not a hard class, midterm/final was just mcq, the rest of your grade is group dependent and some attendance marks near the end of the sem. Overall, the lectures aren't very useful in terms of learning the actual testable course content, prof is engaging but mostly just yaps about personal stories. Just self test with gpt from slides and textbook.

Prof was very approachable and wanted the best for her students. Group work does take up around 30% of ur grade, but not difficult. Exams are definitely more difficult, but are all mc (very concept based). I'd recommend using quizlet and using chat to make more based on the practice questions given. Lectures are very similar to textbook content.

This professor spends majority of her lecture talking about herself, she will very rarely speak about examinable content… you are better off just studying the text book and praying she doesn't put any bad questions on the exam

I have never questioned the qualifications of a professor until taking this class. Sure she has many degrees which she mentions every class, however she doesn't actually teach or understand how boring her classes are. She talks about herself and life for 70% of the class and only spending about 30% of the time talking about actual course ideas.
Prof wasn't bad but the exams were bad even though they were multiple choice the answers were all the same aside from one word.
One of the worst professors at Beedie. She doesn't know what she's talking about and puts little effort into creating effective evaluations. Just bad.

Professor Assadi gives incredible lectures, she is funny, but doesn't veer off topic much. Go to the lectures, there are bonus points that go toward your final. Speaking of her exams, they are incredibly disorganized and confused weirdly. Plus, her group assignments SUCK. She's amazing, but the course structure/exams needs serious revamping.
Midterm was pretty hard, lots of tricky questions.

Dr. Assadi had decent lectures. At times, it got boring but she'd use videos and class participation to make it more engaging. Make sure you study well for the exams as a majority of the grades are on there and its fairly easy to study for if you put effort using flashcards and memorization and really understanding the concepts.

Good teacher. As long as you read and understand textbook/lectures you are going to do great! The group projects are kinda annoying but you just gotta deal with it. Other than that it was an okay class, lectures would just get boring.

Super nice prof! Class was based on 2 group projects, tutorial attendance, a midterm and a final (non cumulative). Memorize everything from the slides including examples, and understand the concepts to be able to apply in scenario based questions. Textbook is a lot of reading, majority of the content is already on the slides.
At times this class was extremely easy and at times I wanted to drive off a cliff because of it.

I don't know how people are complaining are so much that final was very easy if you just read all the lectures even like one time. Don't get thrown off by negative reviews, prof is completely fine, it's just the actual content is so boring.
Just avoid at all costs

The midterms and finals are absolutely brutal including random excerpts from examples in lecture as questions. Papers are graded brutally and the TA didn't seem to actually put in much effort. Prof seems superficially nice but I cannot say one good thing about this course.
Everyone, please choose Prof.Assadi's 272/381 class please! I took both with her, she is really a great one for all of the HR faculty!!!!
Best prof ever for all the BUS272 Instructors!
She is so nice! When I talked to her about the concerns about the exams, she comforted me and really gave a lot of useful tips to write and review the exams!!! Thank you Bahareh!
This lady is the best prof for BUS272, with not too many team works and an easier workload for the course! As a lower-division student, I highly recommend.
Really nice Prof, compare to the E100 section, she is the GOAT!!
Awful Professor, told by her that "as long as I did the peer view, you will receive full mark". But by the end, she didn't follow her word. Hope she can one day be disqualified by the SFU. Ewwww

Midterms and finals have 3 versions. One is easy while two are extremely difficult, hence resulting in a high curve. If you get a bad group not much will be done. The ones with the harder paper will always get a bad grade. Please just avoid if you want a good grade.

Bahareh was a great professor! If you want to do well in her course remember to assist to the tutorials! Also, if you participate in her class I think it will count as your participation grade and it will add up so much towards your grade! Although participation is not mandatory during her class, it is strongly encouraged!

there's so many reviews saying that she's not a good prof, so I was worried when I took this course with her, but she's a great prof I think people are just lazy and don't study and then blame it on the prof :/. She gives extra credit each class if you participate, her lectures are also really interesting and she always ends class early

Dr. Assadi is a wonderful professor. She cares about her students and gives lots of opportunities for extra credit. She is very friendly and makes her lectures a very welcoming space for students to participate. She is hard marker but if you read the textbook and really understand the information you will do just fine in her class.

I didn't care much about the lectures, but the tutorials were where I learnt the most. Simply because the smaller cohorts made it easier for me to discuss ideas/theories with my peers. The TA helped me with understanding the material and setting me up for success. I really valued the prompt email response I got, especially prior to the midterm. Ty

She is super funny and makes sure to brighten up the dry material that tends to come with business. The work was hard, but if you attend all lectures and tutorials, and do the hw then it isn't hard to come up with a solid mark. The only thing that was hard was the group projects. Just depends which teammembers you get!

Terrible class. For both BUS 272 and BUS 237. She seems to just read off the lecture slides. Moreover, answers written on exams were worded a little differently from what she had on the answer sheet and you don't get any marks for it. Same meaning, just different words. If you can choose any other prof, take them.
Totally awesome Professor, if you really want to learn something other than hitting powerpoint slides and trying to get an easy A, this is your guy.  Learned more in this class than any other course
The prof is cool, the lecture is interesting but he is not very clear about the projects.

Talks about random stuff for the entire time... talks on the phone during entire tutorial... tells you what chapters are on the midterm one week before its due .. self teach yourself everything basically
Best teacher ever. His lectures are so informative that he doesn't even need to open the PowerPoint. His lectures about life are much more important than the course content.

Absolutely amazing prof! Passionate and interesting to listen to. Both "quizzes" and assignments are easy. No midterm or final, just 3 MC quizzes. No need to attend lecture (although they are super interesting), everything on quizzes is from textbook and written assignment is your choice. Hardest part is the group project.

by far the worst prof in sfu. opens a power point slide, and out of ~40 slides he stays on the first slide which has the topic of the lecture for half the class. even though we pointed out that we'd like him to relate more to the textbook, he still talks about useless random stuff that is very hard to relate to the textbook. avoid at all cost

Lectures are so boring, prof talks way too much about useless stuff and doesn't even go over the course material. instructions for assignments and group projects are so vague, everyone didn't know what to do. Easy class but pretty much a gamble with your grades if you get stuck with lazy people for group project.

The most useless but easiest Business class @ sfu. TA taught most of the class. Grading was odd,  high averages, easy "quizzes", no need to attend classes b/c tests based solely on text, cramming day of exam will give you an A easily. Don't bother going to some tut, esp if you live far. P.S. There are no bonus marks, don't do assessments. Hoax.
Boriest & Useless busines class ever. Useless lecture, striaght from the textbook. Quiz was striaght from the book too!

This course was super easy. Lecture notes useless, Study straight from the textbook, exams done from the textbook, use textbook multiple choice pratice. Lots of bonus work, final worth 8%, make sure to get above class average on all projects or you'll end up with a c. class average was around 90%.

Fun class, entertaining professor, but REALLY annoying assessments, and you never knew if he was actually going to show up for class. Graded on a curve, so you have to do extra well on tests in order to get a good mark because they are so easy.

The one and only reason you should take this course with this prof is to have a super easy final, which is pretty good if you have many.However, the marking scheme is very unfair, the professor does not even come to his lectures (with no explanations as to why) and group work sucks when your randomly picked group is full of lazy people.grade=random

Material and lectures were interesting and not difficult to understand, but i found that (with the way marks were distributed) I got a terrible grade. It lowered my GPA quite a bit, I was surprised. Group work counted for far too much I think.

It seems like an easy class.. its sorta common sense and psychology combine together. His lectures are useless. No one listens to him. This class is basically self learned and self read. He gives alot of bonus marks and makes u do a bunch of these assessments. Quiz and finals arent worth that much but presentation have to do well!
Good class / prof.

No fun at all, he teaches nothing in the class,(I guess there is nothing to teach about,I just hate this course,the whole orgnization behavior is nonsense,nohting pratical!!)so much time spend on assessment and team meetings(at least three times effert to put in then other courses) but so little you will receive.Everyone is disappointed.

waste of time doing all those assessments, bad teaching, waste of time going to class, final is just like the 3 quizzes=8% each, average is too high, worst instructor, seems like an easy class but its not

Marking scheme is horrible, as are TAs (well, at least one). Therefore it's a lottery with your grade. Another thing is with the surveys - the prof really annoys everyone with them. He is doing some research, but gee, with the way the students answer these, the only valid research is about pseudo-random number generation.

It's like this, you only need your textbook for 4 small 10% quizzes. The rest of the marking is based on luck, so if you feel lucky, take this course with Coetzer. And he will make you fill out probably 20 hours with of assessments over the course. Attending lectures and getting a high grade are negatively correlated in my opinion. Take it w/Zatsik
its seems like an easy course and all but the average is way too high.  His lectures are okay, but the assessments get repetitive

A nice guy that likes to hear himself talk. Don't go to lecture, there is no point as he doesn't test you on anything but the text. Make the class average way too high so when you think your doing well you most likely arn't doing super great. 'Nice I got 92% on my paper! Oh crap the average is 90%'

I got an A+ and didn't go to a single lecture. Study the text book and do a good job in the group work and you'll cruise on easy street. By the way....too many damn surveys I just started answering randomly after the first few, were a waste of time for me and the Prof.
No Comments
fun guy but dont expect him to be helpful, hes lazy and wont do as he says

lectures have nothing to do with tests but he makes them interesting and understandable.. although u could get a great mark in this class without going to a single lecture his lectures provide great insight about life
Very dynamic and animated; he marks too easy though.  The class averages were really high so you didn't do as well as you really should have.
No Comments
No Comments
Very very nice. Crystal clear lectures but can be boring. Exams are okay. The research paper was the only bad thing in this course as they marked it pretty hard but I loved the course.

Class is boring and everything is tested out of the textbook, after the first MT no one came to class. Study and memorize the textbook, but her M/C questions on her exams are worded tricky. It's a pretty easy course, but honestly, it's hard to make it interesting, she tries to do a decent job, I went to all lectures just to save study time later
sorry, i typed bus 237 in the previous rating, but i do mean bus 272. i don't want people to think that that comment was for another prof, it WAS from brenda lautsch.
She's a great prof. Had her few years back. Very Hot.
Course material is easy but boring! Lectures were a drag and no one attended. Memorizing the textbook is the way to do well on tests. Quite useless and irrelevant.

SHE WAS ONE GREAT PROF! Always prepared, knows her stuff really well, makes the class INTERESTING!! (apparently thats a hard thing for many profs to do!) overall great course and an awesome professor so if your thinking of taking this class I say GO FOR IT! An easy A

She's a very clear and understanding prof and tries to make lectures interesting even though the material is very boring. She gives lots of interesting examples. I felt bad for her that half the class leaves during the break and I think she knows it too.Course material is not difficult at all, just memorize the textbook and you'll do well on exams.
Never went to the class(except for the 1st & last class) and managed to get an 'A' by studying the text book. OB is the most boring class I've ever been to.
Her personality was good, she tries to make a useless course interesting.  The exams are very straight forward.  You don't need to attend lectures though, you get all you need from the textbook.
i really liked this course, it was straight forward and practicle. go to lectures though.. questions on finals may have only been discussed in lecture, not textbook.
easy class... take it with her
Buuuuuuu
agreed. the class is easy but you should go to her lecture.  there was a question on final exam out of 10 that was covered in class, not in the textbook.
dont bother going to class..enough said

Her lectures are nothing more than what's exactly in the text book.  Kind of a waste of time.  Without even trying I got 80% on the midterm, however since the class is on a curve that's still only a B-...its easy if you need a business class but kinda boring
Lectures may not be too useful, but they're interesting and she tries to make them fun.  Make sure to memorize the textbook & this will be one of the easiest courses you'll ever take.
Class is pretty straight forward. memorize the text, and youll do good. mostly MC questions, and 2 long answer question. So far the easiest business class I took.

She is a clear prof, and give interesting material to explain the book in lecture, and lecture gives out hints for final written questions. Midterm is easy with class avg 78%, if u wanna do good, getta do well on Group paper. The tutorial is stupid>&qu

Very nice prof, she tries her best to help you, and not to mislead you. Lectures were a bit boring though. As well, the term paper marking was unfair, she went too tough on it. Somehow, most groups got the same mark...73%...hmmm.
She's a great lecturer, although the skits near the end of the course were pretty lame.  If you did well in PSYC 100/102, you'll do well in this course.

Nice prof! Easy class! Must study the textbook cuz test is based on the text. Lectures are pretty useless but going to lectures will help u answer the paragraph questions on the final. Tutorial is uselss but you get parti. mark for it

This class is extremely easy.  Just memorize the text and the tests are a piece of cake.  There is no point of going to class or tutorial (except for tutorial participation).  There is no advantage in going to class, tests are right from the text.
Nice lady! only one group paper, TAs are quite nice too. Overall class Avg is too high but if you study the textbook you should do pretty well on her exams. The exams are mainly m/c.

I think the lectures are quite useful.  Since lectures are textbook based, I found it useful when I study for the exams.  Hint: take notes in class!!!  While other 272 classes require personal papers, by taking her class, you need only write 1 grp paper.
skits!!! what the heck is that about....this is university u know...JEZ
The turials are unnecessary. She is a good proffesor, and tries to attrac the attetion of students during her class, but in fact people don't ussually go often to the classes

She is a nice lady, but I dont get why everyone thought it was an easy class.. I thought it was pretty difficult even though I attended all the lectures and tutorials.. Especiall the multiple choice part of exams.
Lectures and tutorials are pretty pointless.  Memorize the textbook and her notes.  That's all there is to her exams.  I didn't attend lectures and did well.
Best prof ever, fun and helps you to learn very boring material in as exciting a way as possible.

She's very boring. It's hard to get excited in the class especailly when we have to do skits infront of the class.The textbook covers everything you need to know. Read it well cause questions come from there!Easy common sense course.
NO USE TO GO TO LECTURES. just memorize the book. Lectures are more like a high school drama class.
Worst prof I ever had. Waste time on Group activity. Poor lecture notes. No sample exams to practice with. Just read the text. Lecture and tutorial are both Absolute waste of time.
"hey, there's still an hour left in lecture... let's do a skit."
No Comments
No Comments
easy prof, easy course, boring lesson though (common sense)
RUN!!!!!! Choose another prof instead of him please!

He is a great professor: really cares for all his students, has so many amazing stories to share. As long as you memorize his slides, it's very easy to get full marks on midterms and finals. Get ready for a lot of assignments: that's where I feel I lost some marks. I definitely recommend taking a class with him. Best prof so far!
His lectures are inspiring but lecture notes are lacking.  You need the textbook to make sense of things
Really great prof. Class was enjoyable. Hope to take him again.

Sam was very inspirational and was also quite passionate about teaching. The issue was that he wasn't too interested in the content he was teaching. He would often spend a fraction of the lecture teaching the content and the rest talking things to inspire the class. You can do well without going to lectures just make sure to read the textbook.

Sam is a very good professor who is really passionate about the subjects he teaches, and really wants to help students reach their full potential. His exams are very fair, with no unexpected content and a focus on demonstrating understanding of the concepts in a real world scenario, not just focusing on writing down the exact definition.
Midterm and finals were pretty straight forward. Group and pair projects pretty much make or break your grade. Sam is a great lecturer and will go the extra mile to help you out.

amazing person and knowledgable, but lectures can be pretty boring, i stopped going after the first few weeks. ur grade is based on lots of group project, so if u get assigned with a bad group, it will be hard to get a good grade. u dont get most of ur marks back till end of semester, so u dont know ur true grade

Bill Scott is a tremendous professor. He is friendly, approachable, and he cares about his students. His lectures are quite informative and inspirational. He answers emails in good time and makes the work clear. There are quite a few assignments in BUS 272, but they are fair and explained well for the most part. I would recommend Professor Scott

for 3credits, there was ALOT of assignments. besides the weekly quizzes, there was always sum to do almost every week, sometimes the assignment would overlap and you end up doing 2 things. they're all group work so if you end up with bad members, you'll do bad. you'll end reading the whole textbook cuz his sides are bad. He's a good teacher tho

Very friendly and responds to emails quickly. Exams are fair but wording can be vague--focus on applying concepts as exams are all written application questions and no MC. Bill is clearly passionate about teaching and cares for his students, def take it w/ him (he made a wheel of fortune game to make lectures more fun and engaging for students)! :)
Bill Scott is a solid choice. I took BUS 272 online and the course structure is quite simple and easy. Overall the course is quite basic/simple.

pair projects and group projects. course concepts are fairly easy to understand. however you need to actually know what you're learning about to do well. memorization does not help. he's a nice guy and his lectures are interesting. a good beginner business course

Very "liberal" course. Loads of group projects and in class activities. The concepts were not difficult, weekly quizes were easy and the professor and TA marked assignments fair. The mid term and final are largely multiple choice and honestly kind of tricky.
Worst professor ever had. His lecture is useless and the PowerPoint are useless at all! Dont take his class. It is chaotic!

Exams aren't clear specially case study. I couldn't understand her expectation in the group assignment. very strict in marking. She can be interesting but what is the point is she isn't clear and if she is strict on marking.

She's a very nice young lady. Ph.D. candidate, updated. She's very clear about course content, and you will probably enjoy the class. But if you can, take the tutorial with other TAs. She's a hard marker, and although I got an A, I have to say this, the curve is pretty weird. Work hard and pursue the highest standard. Few things are predicable.
Very good teacher, always willing to help and very fair to all students throughout the term. I recommend her. Tests aren't hard they are mostly based on the slides.

Stacy is the best prof ever! I took OB with her last semester and I absolutely loved the course because she was very helpful and passionate about what she was teaching. Make sure to memorize pretty much everything she teaches in lectures and you will ace the class. Midterms and Finals are straight forward.
made the course interesting!

Great prof. Lots of energy. She is young and tries hard to make the material interesting, tells funny personal stories and shows videos. Only recommend the class if you are in business. Not as easy as it seems.
Very active lecture and tutorial, makes the subject really interesting. Very clear on what is expected, and exams were very fair. Best professor I have met so far.

Stacey is an awesome prof! She's super enthusiastic and gives great feedback on your work. Tutorials with her were fun! The midterm and final were really easy but the class ave was so high so I didnt do very well. Even though I didnt do well, she made me love the class. Only thing I hated was the case study.

The midterm and final exam questions looked pretty straight forward, but because of that, the class average before final was super high . I had spent alot of time on this course and i did very well on the midterm and the case report. but i ended up getting only a B for the course.
enthusiastic professor! Definitely recommended
Stacey actually seemed to care about the subject. That's kinda rare for profs.
she is GREAT !! however, it's not easy to get high marks
She is AWESOME!!! My only pet peeve is the exams are a bit tough.

All his examples are real life example from his on experience. Not only are they funny, they are completely related to the concepts he is talking about thereby making them easy to understand. All in all, he made a boring course based on memorizing, fun.

AWESOME prof. super laid back and chill. you can tell he really enjoys the material. gives alot of examples that help you understand the material. very fair exams. lots of opportunities to get marks. very entertaining class. study and memorize the material & you'll do great!

Very interesting and funny to relate real life examples to explain slides. TA is also nice. However, multiple choice can be ambiguous and he only takes direct textbook ideas for written. You'll be lucky to get couple marks trying to explain in your own words. So it seems easy, but scores are low and marking really tough, even for tutorial projects.
He's an outgoing and nice guy, however I think his short answer questions on the midterm were EXTREMELY vague. Basically, if you memorize the textbook, you'll do great in this class.

Ashley Bennington is a bad prof, he is very arrogant and thinks he is better than everyone else especially the people he works with. He rants about his own stories in class trying to relate to the topics but during the exams, does not accept any words other than the ones used in the text book. Simply encourages students to be booksmart.

Really entertaining prof! Lectures are interesting and worth attending. His assignments and tests are fair. You will do well as long as you studied a bit. The curve can pull you down sometimes, but overall great prof!

He is a great prof, classes are entertaining so you are never bored in his class. Shows alot of interest in what he is teaching, the only problem is the curve which can mess u up. Other than that great prof and would like to take a class with him again
Lectures are often highly entertaining though sometimes unprofessional. He is easy to get along with and very approachable.

Very funny, approachable and helpful. If you have to take OB, Ashley is the one you should definitely consider first. He makes otherwise very boring materials interesting. I truly enjoyed his lectures because I have learned a lot and he told us many real life cases which are fascinating.

Ashley is an awesome prof with great interest in the material and great knowledge. MT and Final were fair and easy if you know your concepts. He is also very young which makes him easy to relate to. Overall, the class was one of the only classes I looked forward to going to, even though it was a night class. If you can, definately take his section!

He is an excellent prof. The exam is easy, except for the open-ended part in which he cannot control the difficulty. Very approachable, very HR-like. Lectures are interesting and funny, and he uses real-life examples to interpret course contents!

Ashley changed my university education. He is the only porfessor that I have ever witnessed have such an interest in the things he teaches with giving you a sense of what it is like in the real job market. He swears to keep the idiots in class awake and not make it sound like he's just reading off the notes...not to seem like he's cool. Great class
Great Prof.
Awesome!
The class is pretty easy. His lecture notes helps with studying for exams. Try to get him as your TA as well. Really easy going guy.
awesome prof. funny guy. makes the course interesting. he doesnt swear too much... or at all now that i think of it
awesome and really funny

Ashley is a great prof. teaches textbook and real life information about the course. He  does not cram anything. Midterm was quite straight-forward as he outlined very clearly the format and content covered. Take this class with him!
Awesome prof. Lectures are not just from book; teaches you about the real workplace and stuff.
One of the nicest teachers I've ever had, she even gave out candy every class (I'm not kidding). She was my tutorial leader too, incredibly helpful and went out of her way to help students.

One of the nicest teachers. she is very helpful if you go to her. she makes the class really easy to learn with her given notes. to get an A in her class, go to lectures and tutorial, read her lecture notes, and read only parts of the book that she doesnt cover in lecture. she is not hard to understand, although she has the classis asian accent LOL

Extremely helpful in her office hours. Really kind. Her lectures are what's going to be on the exam so there is no deception there. Never miss tutorials. It's a really easy course, but don't get fooled and fall behind.  Overall excellent prof. If you can, take the course with her.

Her lectures are extremely dry and boring. Lectures are  the same as the book... even the examples she uses are the same. Hard to stay awake in lecture because her accent is very, very heavy, English grammatical errors in her speech and she speaks really slowly. Just memorize the textbook for the tests. Tutorials were hard though, don't skip that.
Nice prof! The course material is easy and so are the exams; simply memorize the slides and go to every classes..But the tutorial is desperate...tutorial leading and case analysis are difficult.
There's A LOT of work in her class. But her lecture is interesting so far. Echo uses many photos and videos in the lecture. She is also kind and patient.

Michael did everything he could to be engaging, I found it to be too much. There were constant surveys, polls, and stories in class that felt like I never knew when to actually write down or pay attention to what he was saying

Beyond useless. Poor excuse for a lecturer. Way below sfu standards. Students deserve their money back if they took the course with him. Could have just red the hbr articles he assigned and done well on the final. Waste of money. This prof cant even finish a sentence without stuttering and correcting himself. Then does useless polls.

RUN AND DROP this class if you want to take OB as required. Throughout the semester, quizzes and poll were all I can remember. If you want to learn something and get at least a good grade, GO TO OTHER PROFS

DO NOT TAKE CLASS WITH HIM! It will be time-consuming and waste lots of your money if you take the class with him, also you will feel stressed about his horrible teaching. Be aware of his videos because the exam will ask for it! Recommend to drop his class immediately otherwise your GPA will be ruined tbh

HORRIBLE lectures, he doesn't teach anything. He doesn't use his own lecture slides, he just uploads Pearson's "lecture slides"to canvas which is just the same points in the textbook but in a powerpoint. He brings nothing of value, and the only reason people attended his lectures were because he had random pop quizzes throughout the semester.

Nice guy who is super passionate however USELESS lectures, can't communicate, horrible grading as in hes oddly specific about what he wants despite there being a clear rubric and he wont tell you where to improve "because he wants you to do it by yourself". Irrelevant lectures, horrible marking, horrible communication, RUN.
Blazes through slides, super nice guy but content seems empty, will spend lots of time on obvious questions that no one wants to answer instead of on testable material

Michael was a very knowledgeable prof in terms of BUS 272 and tried his best to encourage class participation in every lecture, even when there were only 12 people in the lecture hall. All his exams are multiple choice, just do the pearson online questions and you will be fine.
The fairest and most understanding prof I have had to date. Her lectures were above average and it felt like she was walking you through the course content.

Lisa is a really nice prof, I recommend taking her for 272 as she gets straight to the content. Lecture attendance isn't mandatory, but tutorials are (marks for participation). Group presentation, and mini written assignments add to marks. Midterm and Final are based off slides + text, and are similar to the Mylab questions.

Very kind professor who is willing to help students succeed. Her lectures are entertaining and help with understanding the course content. Class is entirely based on textbook so focus on that when studying for exams. Overall a super nice prof who cares about her students.

Lisa is kind, helpful, and creative in the way she explains stuff. She encourages people to speak up and share stories. Tutorials are fun and interesting. The textbook is your best friend nevertheless. Grading criteria are a little unclear for midterm & final. Attendance is worth 20% so you can not miss lectures/tutorials at any cost.
Lisa was super nice, assignments and tests were straightforward and based off material. The content was somewhat interesting. Overall a good class and a great professor.

Lisa's pretty nice and relatable. She goes through and provides good real-life examples of organizational behavior theories. Tries to engage class by welcoming and posing questions. I found myself bored but I think it was more the content than Lisa's teaching style.
Amazing Prof! Definitely recommended
My favourite professor.I learned a lot and had fun in lecture and tutorial.

Excellent prof! Clear, fair, and helpful. Lectures were great because she made everything so interesting and was able to give examples and relate them to real life. Her exams were very fair - if you understand the material you will be fine (just remember that Bus 272 is curved though). I would definitely take another class with this professor!
Very good prof would take again!

The class was very fun and interesting, not your typical class with dry and boring lectures. However, the Beedie School of Business is hyper-competitive, and as a result, the Bell curve will either save you, or destroy you.  Be prepared and try super hard.

Very engaging, efficient at teaching/explaining, fun demos, and i loved tutorials becuz of the competitions! just an overall awesome prof. I wish all my profs could be like her. she is what sfu profs should strive for. always enthusiastic and cares very much about student success. TAKE OB WITH HER!

Very nice teacher and super friendly! Her exams are pretty easy as  long as you read her lecture slides and the textbook. I would advise you to attend every lecture as she will have some questions on  the exams based on lectures only. We play fun games in tutorial and are rewarded with points for winning.hated this aspect because my team never won)
Nicest teacher I've ever had. Super likeable and friendly. Class is easy, as long as you're above the curve. So awesome!
nice and easygoing

Her lectures are Extremely boring and hard to listen to. Lots of memorization so do the readings. Cant even find the free version of the textbook so had to buy it. So many term to learn and not many assignments or opportunities to bring up your mark. She talks about whats covered on the test in class so go to lectures

Incredibly dry class, but Melissa tries her best to make it engaging. Lots of content and information to memorize for midterm and final, so read the textbook well beforehand. The content itself is not difficult, just lots of information and terms to memorize. Fairly easy class if you're good at memorization.
Loved Melissa for 272! TA was also awesome. Tests are heavy though, prepare to study lecture slides and read the text. Highly recommend!

The worst 272 prof across the Bby and Surrey campus. Dragged lectures too long making it extremely hard to stay awake and alert, and excessively difficult midterm and final compared to other classes. Emphasis on the excessivley! DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS!
She is so bad for BUS272, come on! How's your exam looks like that? Worst Prof I have ever met!

if you have a choice don't choose her as ur prof. final was so hard, nothing could prepare you unless u remember everything from every lecture leading up to it. final was cumulative and was prolly one of the worse written exams ive ever wrote. suppose to be easier class but she made it harder than needed. she's a nice person tho. but DONT TAKE IT
Meh.  Not the best, not the worst.  I'll probably forget the whole class by next year.
He is nice.

Lieke is a great prof! Her lectures are very interesting, she includes videos & examples which is helpful but also you wouldn't expect those to pop up on tests but they can. Her lecture slides have all the info you need, but the text book goes into detail so the choice is yours. class is curved and av. was rlly high but overall great class!!

Was doing fine until the team assignment. She and the TA's went hard on the grading, and completely destroyed some people's mark. I dropped in total of 3 grades from a B+ down to a C. Absolutely pissed right now. Avoid her class unless you wanna see your mark go from a high down to low because of the harsh grading.
Lieke is awesome, she really cares about her students. If you are having difficulty in her class, just ask! She will go above and beyond to make sure you understand

The best prof i never met before. Really care about her students. This course is not hard, especially if you take this course with lieke. Make sure take this course with lieke, because you will have much less work.

Lieke clearly cares about her work, however she doesn't give super interesting lectures. This class has quite a bit of work to it but you'll do fine by going to her lectures and knowing the material from the slides. Also, semester-long group project.

Lieke is honestly an amazing prof, everything is straight forward and follows her lecture slides. Hard to find a prof that's actually passionate on what they teach and shows it in the lecture. Lots of terms and memorization but I found everything related to each other. Textbook is supplementary but definitely can help bump you an extra letter grade

This course is an easy A although the average is very high. As long as you follow her lecture slides and understand the main idea behind each theory, you are good. Just beware of team assignment and spend time working on it. Your grades depend on TA too

Lieke is a very average business prof. Her lectures  can be boring and are not mandatory. Her exams are super easy. I got an A in the course with 88% and minimal effort. Average was a bit lower then I expected but still kind of high. Textbook not mandatory, but supplements the lecture slides which can bump you up a letter grade.

Not only one of the best professors I've had, but also one of the best people I have met. Lieke is devoted, passionate, humble, and truly always ready and willing to help. Get to know her, and use her energy and availability to make the most out of her course. You will gain deep and wide awareness on the psychological basics of business.

Sweetest prof you'll meet. Lectures can be boring as she most of the information can be found in the book but she'll add in tidbits of information not inside the book. Tests contain stuff from textbooks and her lectures. Assignments are difficult, and grading criteria is pretty ambiguous.

Ron is a passionate instructor who is dedicated to unlocking the leadership potential in his students. He empowers his students by delivering thought-provoking lessons that delves into the emotional process people experience in a change initiative and the tools that future leaders need to keep our teams focused and productive.
Not sure if you attended the same class. Made you work hard but fair in feedback and grade

Doesn't teach. always finishes the class half-1 hour before 2 hours is up. doesn't reply to emails. doesn't show up on midterm and final exams. very irresponsible.  he also requires us to read an additional boring book...
I thought Ginger had a great teaching method. I would attend lectures just to hear her stories. In order to ace this class memorize the text and you'll be set!
I really enjoyed this course. The material is mainly memorizing the textbook but Ginger made it as interesting as she could.
Don't know what's wrong with previous comments - I found her excellent! Helpful and great attitude towards students. But, I am getting good marks in the course!

What can I say, Ginger is very condescending to the students in this class. She literally treats everyone like a child, assumes everyone doesn't know anything and couldn't care less about her students. I know she's a great prof in upper level courses, but it'll be great if her attitude was the same. Oh and memorize the textbook!
Enjoyed the course - she does the best she can with this material. Mandatory bell curve can make it a nightmare so be prepared!
She did not seem interested in teaching, only the subject matter and listening to herself speak.

I was very interested in this course; at the end, it was not what I was expecting. We watched movies all semester. She just added 3 more chapters for final on last week of semester. I don't think she and TA teach anything. I personally didn't learn anything from her class except watching several movies. Don't take her class...Also she always late.
She's good because she relates the material, but in all honesty I found the lectures that weren't movies to be a little boring, and a tad repetitious,
Great prof
HE is not helpful,so bad

I feel that Phyllis is the most disorganized, boring and useless professor I have ever experienced. I did exceptionally well on all assignments, as reflected in my WEBCT percentage of 85%. I was sure I would get at LEAST a B. So, due to her teaching inabilities, my grade was bell-curved so far down as to reflect a C- for 85%, I was surprised!!!
Terrible control over class. Received a mid 80's grade but finished class with a C+. Worst class ever. Easy, but somehow detrimental to GPA. Doesn't make sense.
She will ruin your life!

Prof and TA's are super disorganized and lazy.  Our marks are never updated on webct (Dec 15th today and still havent been updated).  It's so fustrasting just thinking about this class.  Ignore at all costs.

A disorganized prof teaches organizational behaviour. Lectures are not helpful at all. The MT had MC and written questions. Final had 12 written questions. She's the worst prof I have ever had.I don't recommend her.

Awful prof, not helpful at all and her lectures are pretty much useless unless you want to sleep through it. Also very disorganized, even the TAs were confused about what was happening. I recommend avoiding this prof at all costs.

Unhelpful lectures & notes, but fair midterm. monotone puts people to sleep. final is all written questions, which will require u to utilize ur brain for memorizing all the definitions and theories. Doesn't know how to use a computer, wasted 15 minutes of class time to show a video clip which is totally not related to the course.
No Comments
Very soft-spoken so you are instantly put to sleep... Lectures are basically not very helpful... Just stick to reading the textbook...

If you want a high mark with minimal effort, take her courses.  If you want to learn, don't take her courses. She doesn't really seem that motivated to really teach the course as it should be taught. Depends what you want I guess.

Masood is awesome! the course is hard because of the content, but he tries very hard to teach us what we need to know (enough for the exams) there is no surprises with the exams, but you REALLY need to know the material well... the prob is that every gets a good mark, and after the bell curve, you can get a B with 80%! GET KAREN MAY FOR YOUR TA!!
